title: Passive Limit-Order Entry Mode (Behind-the-Quote Placement)
summary: For high-conviction entries (signal score >= threshold), submit a passive limit order 1–2 ticks inside the spread rather than an aggressive market order, with a configurable timeout (e.g. 30–120 seconds) before converting to Adaptive Algo market. This exploits the retail trader's structural advantage: small orders face minimal adverse selection, and behind-the-quote limit orders have the lowest implementation shortfall for retail participants according to the 2024 SSRN/Oxford study.
expected_impact: Oxford/SSRN 2024 study across 19 US retail brokers found limit orders incur measurably lower trading costs than marketable orders, robust to controls for stock, time, size, and direction. Estimated 3–10 bps cost reduction per fill for liquid names.
